title: Xtra Rich German Salmon Braise
description: A cultured and velvety German dish featuring salmon in a rich braise, perfect for adventurous eaters, and egg-free-friendly for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Melt the butter in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the white wine and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the fish stock, heavy cream, dried dill, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the salmon fillets to the pot, skin side up, and spoon some of the braise over the top of each fillet.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven, and braise for 12 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and check the salmon for doneness, then return it to the oven for an additional 2 minutes if necessary.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly, then serve the salmon with the rich braise spooned over the top.
